name: Grid Unique Paths : DP on Grids (DP8)
statement:
Given two integers m and n, representing the number of rows and columns of a 2d array named matrix. Return the number of unique ways to go from the top-left cell (matrix[0][0]) to the bottom-right cell (matrix[m-1][n-1]).


 Movement is allowed only in two directions from a cell: right and bottom.
constraints:
• 1 <= n, m <= 100
  • The answer will not exceed 109

examples.explanation: There are 3 unique ways to go from the top left to the bottom right cell. 1) right -> down -> down 2) down -> right -> down 3) down -> down -> right

There are 4 unique ways to go from the top left to the bottom right cell. 1) down -> right -> right -> right 2) right -> down -> right -> right 3) right -> right -> down -> right 4) right -> right -> right -> down
